signals.reasoning: BTC is constructive, but the edge is still too mild to justify a fresh directional call in a defensive macro regime.
ETH remains the cleanest long because price and open interest are rising together while funding is still flat, which supports a continuation long without obvious crowding.
SOL still reads as a short-covering rally rather than a durable trend continuation, so I would not force a fresh position.
NOT is showing mild squeeze potential with predicted funding easing lower, but liquidity is still too poor to trust for a serious fresh position.
AERO is stable but not showing enough structural or crowding edge to justify a fresh trade here.
FARTCOIN is up 11.9% and still trend-supported, so it remains a valid crowded-upside fade candidate even though the edge is weaker than TAO.
TAO is the clearest fade on this board, with price up 7.94%, funding still elevated near +1.87 bps, and open interest up 20.88%, which leaves the long side crowded.
VVV is still extended, but the funding has normalized and the move still looks like chop near highs rather than a clean cliff-drop setup.
TON is too strong to fade and too extended to chase, so it stays on the watchlist for a future peak rejection instead of a fresh entry.
